Tools: ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Deepseek, Qwen, Grok, Microsoft Copilot
RuleDescription: Entering private, proprietary, or client information into general chat programs is prohibited without an authorized corporate agreement. This includes not entering PII, passwords, API credentials, or internal financial data into any chat tool.

Tools: ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Deepseek, Qwen, Grok, Microsoft Copilot
RuleDescription: Before being implemented in formal deliverables or external communications, all AI-generated outputs must be evaluated by human review.

Tools: ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Deepseek, Qwen, Grok, Microsoft Copilot
RuleDescription: AI involvement must be disclosed when using AI-generated content in legal, compliance, or regulatory submissions.

Tools: ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Deepseek, Qwen, Grok, Microsoft Copilot
RuleDescription: Employees must not use unlicensed or consumer accounts, such as personal Microsoft Copilot, for business purposes. They are also responsible for verifying the accuracy of any AI-generated content before taking action.

Tools: ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Deepseek, Qwen, Grok, Microsoft Copilot
RuleDescription: Only general personal research is permitted using Deepseek and Qwen. Don't utilize it for any projects or tasks related to business.
Proprietary source code that contains client data, credentials, or business logic should not be pasted into public LLM interfaces.
Standard code review is required for all AI-generated code intended for production deployment. The code that a developer commits is their responsibility.
Code generated by AI may contain archaic functions, faulty logic, or security concerns. Don't ever deploy without testing and review.
In AI-generated code, never hardcode credentials, secrets, or API keys. Make use of authorized secret management and environment variables.
Before being deployed, all workflows handling confidential data must receive IT Security approval.
A human review step in the workflow architecture is necessary for automated processes that impact financial data or customer information.
For organizational workflows, avoid using a personal account. Only use accounts belonging to organizations.
Workflows should never be connected to any third-party services or APIs without prior IT security authorization.
Use solely for public research. Never fill out a query with private, client, or internal company information.
Before using responses in reports, documentation, or business decisions, always check them against referenced sources.
For legal, compliance, competitive, or strategic research, do not utilize Perplexity entirely.
Never use personal accounts; only use ones for work. No AI prompt should contain customer information, contract terms, confidential content, or specific employee information.
Before being published or sent, all AI-generated papers, SOPs, and communications must be examined and approved by the appropriate process owner, HR Manager, or Department Head.
AI is unable to comprehend corporate dynamics or existing connections. Before utilizing any AI-drafted output, always modify the tone, content, and context using your own understanding.
Communications concerning disciplinary actions, redundancy, or potentially sensitive organizational changes should not be generated by AI; full human creation and legal consent are required.
Before being published, the Department Head must approve any AI-generated content intended for external or client distribution.
It is possible to mislead AI-generated voices and faces for actual people. A disclosure of AI should always be included in the content description.
Under no circumstances should you create deepfake-style footage of actual people.
